evidence.text: - great pay starting off - lots of amazing benefits like well being subsidy emphasis on well being communities to join expensed networking coffee chats sometimes - learn a lot of consulting 101 and how to be a good leader
great company high salary and good benefits
good pay and health benefits
good pay and benefits lots of talented people at deloitte
good pay good yearly benefits

great work life balance lots of time off for holidays and easy to request pto very flexible on hybrid schedule and required in office days if you want slightly better than ok pay with great flexibility this position is perfect great culture and great people to work with efa sr managers and office leads are very easy to talk too deloitte is a fantastic name to have on resume regardless of position
work from home solid pay and great benefits good work life balance
- great benefits - flexible work-life balance - decent pay bonuses - great portfolio of work in gps sector
- amazing colleagues - high-impact meaningful work - health subsidy - flexible schedule good work-life balance
good work life balance and remote work

- good wlb for pay - leadership varies a lot from project to project but 90 of my experiences have been positive my managers and leadership have been transparent encourage my growth development respectful fair and great to work with - many opportunities to try new work - lots of training and professional development opportunities - good benefits 1000 wellness subsidy maternity paternity leave fertility treatment sup...

-great benefits -remote work -great pay -good opportunity to start your career short term - 2-3 years leverage your experience and what you learn to go elsewhere -an opportunity to challenge yourself -opportunity to earn a bonus every year -deloitte silently promotes individuals to go out into industry and return as a boomerang they will pay you handsomely for the experience you bring back
learned a lot about working for different clients had opportunity to travel for client work and many perks for self care

excellent learning opportunities and professional development the exposure to different industries and clients is invaluable for career growth great benefits package and competitive salary collaborative team environment with smart colleagues who are willing to help prestigious name on resume that opens doors
career growth ample opportunity for development fantastic benefits
internal community of colleagues help give the support needed to get work done compensation pay was med-high

- great colleagues engaging projects if you are in the trendy area ai right now - helpful leaders who have been veterans for decades to guide you - healthy work environment wellness subsidies training - great for overall career development if you don t want to get stuck in purely technical roles
pay social events good stipend and perks
- good networking - great mentors and peers - lots of clear structure and organization - social collaborative environment to grow a range of skill and discover your interest
many opportunities friendly environment too
no pros always politics with everything
good working environment with job security
really good health benefits and good job security
great people decent pay inclusive culture

evidence.location: United States Coast Guard - Air Station Sacramento
evidence.text: good culture and no office politics
doesn't sponosor employment visa for international
raises are not merit based and typically are the same across the entire position
sometimes too hectic lack of good managers that stay on the team lot of chaos lots of changes in the team leaders don't listen they overwork analyst and developers
long hours poorly trained locals as compared to expats such as canadians
if no work is selling in your area of expertise then you have to be willing to do work you might not be good at which will affect your performance review
-not that i can think of
do not get to do work you are passionate about
essentially everything you do is in powerpoint not upfront about additional requirements unclear rules for certain things lack of satisfaction with work
not the best work-life balance not bad a lot of focus on development offshore to india big corporation a lot of mandatory trainings
less pay work responsibilities were not always clear not a work track that supported consultation role
toxic team environments are created by the ts leaders

too much pressure on charge hour goal firm contribution market place timesheet scheduling office utilization etc simultaneously extremely difficult to protect your personal life and to take care your team coworkers are generally very indifferent to each other
huge organization where you will be very little curiosity changes are not well regarded discipline dedication preferred
large corporate politics can impact low level employees
lots of overhead politics and structure that can make simple things complicated
not transparent about project availability and company layoffs
growth does not scale with experience shouldn t be a forever move
as a lifer annual salary increases aren't as competitive as incoming experienced hires
doesn't pay employees fairly work life can be challenging some projects have horrible leaders that micromanage

expected to work on internal projects often multiple in addition to client work analysts - sc are expected to overextend themselves at every opportunity utilization metric limits pto once you hit 120hrs if you take 1 hour more for any reason you will not hit your target cannot bill the extra hours i work because of contract limit analyst - sc are extremely overworked due to toxic hierarchical culture expected to do...
no complains great people and good work environment

not sure if this is a con or not but your career is entirely in your hands there are no automatic promotions or career advancement you need to drive your own success for me this a pro but it's not for everyone
the work isn't always the most meaningful and exciting
consulting does not allow one to fully immerse themselves in the client company or product time with the project can end before realizing the final goal
can't follow industry salary increases
